description_long: This town hall complex features a central rectangular plaza flanked by two large, symmetrical stone buildings with red-tiled gabled roofs and arched colonnades. At one end of the plaza stands a prominent, smaller building with a classical portico supported by columns, likely serving as the main administrative hall. The plaza is paved with patterned stonework, bordered by wide walkways, and the overall layout is highly organized and formal. The stone facades are light-colored, with minimal but distinct ornamentation such as cornices and pilasters. The roofs are pitched and covered with terracotta tiles, adding a warm contrast to the pale stone. The entire structure is elevated on a broad base, suggesting a sense of civic importance and permanence.
description: A grand town hall complex with a central plaza, flanked by two large stone buildings and a columned main hall with red-tiled roofs. The design is formal, symmetrical, and features classical architectural elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main columned entrance hall at the center, with two large symmetrical side buildings and a wide stone plaza.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the plaza, the rear facades of the side buildings, and the layered arrangement of the structures.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the back of the main hall and the symmetry of the two side buildings, with the plaza extending forward.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the main hall from behind, the side buildings, and the patterned paving of the plaza.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Columns, cornices, pilasters, and arched colonnades with minimal additional ornamentation.
attribute_entrance_design: Main entrance features a portico with multiple columns, a set of steps, and likely double doors framed by classical elements.
